product_yields.note: Yxs = mu/qGlycerol = 0.042/0.182 = 0.231 gDCW per mmol glycerol

growth_records.cite: Ramirez-Malule H et al. TCA Cycle and Its Relationship with Clavulanic Acid Production: A Further Interpretation by Using a Reduced Genome-Scale Metabolic Model of Streptomyces clavuligerus. Bioengineering (Basel) (2021). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering8080103. [PMC8389198]
growth_records.method: reported (experimental, 0.042 +/- 0.004 h-1); doubling time calculated td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.042=16.5 h
growth_records.snippet: Growth (h-1) 0.042 +/- 0.004; O2 -1.350; Glycerol -0.182; CO2 1.640; Clavulanate 0.002; Succinate 0.014; Oxaloacetate 0.004; Malate 0.001; Pyruvate 0; Acetate 0 (Table 2, Batch 36 h, Exp column). Flux units in mmol/(gDCW*h).
growth_records.notes: Table 2, Batch (36 h) Exp column, this paper's own fed-batch cultivation of S. clavuligerus DSM 41826 in a 2D rocking bioreactor; footnote: all fluxes experimentally quantified. Uptake table values are negative (consumption); reported here as positive magnitudes. Growth set as hard constraint at experimental value 0.042+/-0.004 h-1. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.042=16.5 h. Yxs=mu/qGlycerol=0.042/0.182=0.231 gDCW/mmol. Batch/production medium composition itself not restated in-text (cited from refs 12,20,43); only feed composition given (glycerol 120 g/L, (NH4)2SO4 8 g/L, K2HPO4 2 g/L). Culture: 28 C, pH 6.8, 0.6 vvm, glycerol C-source.

product_yields.note: Yxs = mu/qGlycerol = 0.031/0.47 = 0.066 gDCW per mmol glycerol

growth_records.cite: Ramirez-Malule H et al. TCA Cycle and Its Relationship with Clavulanic Acid Production: A Further Interpretation by Using a Reduced Genome-Scale Metabolic Model of Streptomyces clavuligerus. Bioengineering (Basel) (2021). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering8080103. [PMC8389198]
growth_records.method: reported (experimental, 0.031 +/- 0.003 h-1); doubling time calculated td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.031=22.4 h
growth_records.snippet: Growth (h-1) 0.031 +/- 0.003; O2 -1.2; Glycerol -0.47; CO2 1.4; Clavulanate 0.004; Succinate 0.007; Oxaloacetate 0.003; Malate 0; Pyruvate 0.005; Acetate 0 (Table 2, Fed-Batch 48 h, Exp column). Flux units mmol/(gDCW*h).
growth_records.notes: Table 2, Fed-Batch (48 h) Exp column, this paper's own cultivation of S. clavuligerus DSM 41826. Footnote: all fluxes experimentally quantified, growth set as hard constraint. Feed constant 0.01 L/h; phosphate-limited stage promoting clavulanic acid (CA) synthesis; max biomass 14.6 gDCW/L at 52 h, max CA 370.9 +/- 14.1 mg/L. Uptake values negative in table (consumption); reported as positive magnitudes. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.031=22.4 h. Yxs=mu/qGlycerol=0.031/0.47=0.066 gDCW/mmol. Production medium composition cited from refs 12,20,43 (only feed given).

Steady-state chemostat so mu=D=0.045 h-1; td=ln2/D=15.4 h. Uptake table values negative (consumption), reported as positive magnitudes. Yxs=mu/qGlycerol=0.045/1.110=0.041 gDCW/mmol. growth_records.medium: GLYCAS-5 (GLYcerol, CAsamino acids and Salts) semi-synthetic medium

up.units: 20 g/L initial, fully depleted by 73 h
up.method: spectrophotometric assay; primary C source, consumed progressively, complete depletion at 73 h coinciding with peak CA
HPLC (OPA/FMOC derivatization); fastest-depleted with Asp/Ser, coincides with glycerol exhaustion

up.units: mg/L time-course (Fig 4A); fully consumed in exponential phase
mg/L; consumed later, coinciding with CA onset

sec.method: HPLC-DAB (imidazole derivatization, 311 nm); peak at end of idiophase
colorimetric Nessler technique; from amino-acid catabolism

mfa.note: dFBA (iLT1021, COBRA) uptake profiles from Monod kinetic model, not independent flux measurements; O2 uptake and CO2 evolution high during EEP/EP then decline (Fig 7B)
CA secretion initiates ~40 h, peaks ~70 h in simulation, matching experiment

fitted_biomass_yield_coeffs_gDW_per_mmol.note: effective fitted parameters (Table 1), not standalone physiological yields

growth_records.cite: Toro-Navarro LF et al. Dynamic metabolic modeling of Streptomyces clavuligerus in complex medium highlights nutrient-dependent metabolic transitions associated with clavulanic acid biosynthesis. PLOS One (2026). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0342057. [PMC12863558]
growth_records.snippet: Batch cultures in GLYCAS-5 yielded a maximum CA concentration of 36.0 +/- 0.52 mg/L at 71 h... a subsequent decline in biomass after reaching 16.3 +/- 0.34 g/L at 42 h. Glycerol, the main carbon source, was rapidly consumed and completely d
growth_records.notes: Batch shake-flask (28 C, pH 6.8, 220 rpm, 140 h, n=3) of S. clavuligerus ATCC 27064 in fully specified GLYCAS-5 medium. Real exometabolome measured: biomass (gravimetric DW), glycerol (spectrophotometric), 16 amino acids (HPLC), phosphate, ammonium, and clavulanic acid (HPLC). Key measured values: max biomass 16.3 g/L at 42 h; glycerol (20 g/L) fully depleted at 73 h; phosphate ~70% consumed (residual 0.76 g/L); ammonium accumulated to 0.15 g/L at 144 h; CA max 36.0+/-0.52 mg/L at 71 h. Growth phases: EEP 0-20 h, EP 21-40 h, SP 41-59 h, DP >=60 h. No per-compound uptake/secretion rates in mmol/gDW/h are given as measured values in text; specific uptake rates in the model come from dFBA/Monod kinetics (COBRA, iLT1021) and are model-derived, not independent measurements. Robustness-analysis nitrogen uptake fixed at 1 mmol/gDW/h is an in-silico constraint, NOT experimental. Biomass yield coefficients (Table 1) are effective fitted parameters.

derived.qs_mu_note: pooled across studies/media — not a single chemostat series, so no maintenance model fitted
